summary: Iteration 18 — Sales Reports now gated by dedicated 'sales_reports' permission (PM no longer inherits via quotations:read) + new /api/admin/role-preview/{role} endpoint feeding live Workspace Preview panel in the Add User dialog. Backend pytest 25/25 PASS (test_iteration18_sales_rbac_role_preview.py): PM gets 403 on all 5 reports endpoints + /api/sales/search; sales_executive and super_admin get 200 on all; PM still has 200 on enquiries/quotations/orders/enquiry-pulse (no regression); /api/admin/role-preview/{role} returns 403 for PM, 9 departments + fallback_all=false for super_admin, 9 departments + fallback_all=true for unmapped role, and only mapped slugs for an explicitly mapped role. Frontend Playwright verified end-to-end (logged in as admin@erp.com): /app/profile shows Add User button, dialog opens in 2-column layout with WorkspacePreview on the right (data-testid='workspace-preview'); selecting super_admin in user-role select live-renders 9 tiles + the green 'Super-admin · sees every department' line (data-testid='workspace-preview-superadmin'); switching to 'vendor' (unmapped) live-renders the amber fail-open warning (data-testid='workspace-preview-fallback') and the superadmin label disappears — confirming live refetch of /api/admin/role-preview/{role} without page reload; each tile has data-testid='workspace-preview-tile-{slug}'; nav-sales-reports is visible for super_admin in sidebar.

issues: Dialog header reads 'New User' but action button reads 'Create' — consistent with existing pattern but a one-line subtitle like 'They will land on the tiles below after sign-in' would make the preview's role more obvious to a new admin.

When switching roles rapidly the WorkspacePreview shows 'Loading…' for ~500ms while previous tiles disappear — consider keeping prior tiles in place and overlaying a small spinner to avoid layout flicker.

action_items: (OPTIONAL/LOW) Keep prior tiles visible while role preview is refetching to avoid 'Loading…' flicker.

(OPTIONAL/LOW) /api/admin/role-preview/{role} does not validate that {role} is a known role string. An unknown/garbage role currently triggers the 'fallback_all' branch which is the desired behaviour, but consider validating against ROLES list and returning 404 (or a flag like 'unknown_role: true') so the UI can show a clearer message than the generic fail-open warning.

(OPTIONAL/LOW) Login page (/login) prefills admin@erp.com / Admin@123 as a 'demo' shortcut. Manual second-account logins still work fine via UI, but the prefill is sticky enough that it sometimes confuses automated re-login flows — consider clearing the field on focus or moving the 'Demo:' hint below the button as a one-click 'Fill demo creds' link.
critical_code_review_comments: rbac.py: sales_reports:read currently grants access to {super_admin, director, general_manager, dept_head, sales_executive, accounts_executive}. Spec says 'project_manager no longer inherits' — confirmed. However, accounts_executive is included in sales_reports:read but NOT in quotations:read, which is inconsistent (a finance role can see pipeline analytics but not the underlying quotations). Confirm intent.

departments_router.role_preview: super_admin short-circuits to list(META.keys()) which depends on dict-insertion order being stable. With Python 3.7+ this is guaranteed but worth a one-line comment so future devs don't sort/filter META keys and accidentally reorder tiles.

departments_router.role_preview: returns fallback_all=true silently when the requested role is genuinely unknown (typo). This is intentional fail-open, but means a typo in the role select silently shows 'all departments' rather than an error — risky UX in a security-adjacent screen. Suggest adding 'known_role: bool' field to the response so the UI can differentiate 'mapped to nothing yet' (legit) from 'role string we don't know' (typo).

Profile.jsx WorkspacePreview: useEffect on `role` dispatches a network call on every keystroke if role were a free-text field; since it's a <select> this is fine, but the component does not cancel the in-flight request when role changes again — fast switching can race and the slower response wins. Add an AbortController or an isMounted/cancel flag.

Profile.jsx WorkspacePreview: error state shows raw e.response.data.detail which for non-super_admin will be 'Super admin only' — but the panel is only rendered inside the Add User dialog which only shows for super_admin, so this is OK. Still, render a friendlier line.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on Add User dialog + WorkspacePreview live updates + super_admin nav-sales-reports visibility. PM/sales_exec nav-sales-reports visibility was not directly observed because the login page's React-controlled prefill of admin@erp.com made automated second-account logins racy; however the nav gate in Layout.jsx uses perm:'sales_reports' which is computed by permissions_for() — and the backend pytest already proves PM=403 / sales_exec=200 / admin=200 on every sales_reports endpoint, so the perm bit sent to the frontend is correct.

test_credentials: admin@erp.com / Admin@123 (super_admin), sales@erp.com / Sales@123 (sales_executive), test_pm@erp.com / PM@12345 (project_manager) — all verified working at the API level.
seed_data_creation: test_mapped_role_scoped will PUT /api/admin/role-department-map to set sales_executive -> ['sales'] if no other mapping exists. This persists across runs. Idempotent.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend: pytest /app/backend/tests/test_iteration18_sales_rbac_role_preview.py -v (needs REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL env var). Frontend: login page prefills admin@erp.com — to switch accounts in automation, either (a) use a fresh context.clear_cookies() AND clear inputs via direct keyboard typing rather than JS setter (React's controlled state seems to reset to 'admin@erp.com' default), or (b) hit POST /api/auth/login directly with requests and then page.context.add_cookies(). Add User dialog testids: add-user-btn, workspace-preview, workspace-preview-superadmin (only for role=super_admin), workspace-preview-fallback (only for unmapped roles), workspace-preview-tile-{slug} for each department, workspace-preview-empty, user-role (select), user-name/email/password/department/phone (inputs), user-save (Create button). New backend endpoint: GET /api/admin/role-preview/{role} — super_admin only, returns {role, departments:[{slug,title,tagline,icon,color}], fallback_all}.
rca of the issue: Not a bug fix — feature/RBAC change. Iteration 17 flagged that PM had quotations:read which leaked pipeline analytics. Iteration 18 (a) added a dedicated 'sales_reports' permission resource in /app/backend/rbac.py mapped to {super_admin, director, general_manager, dept_head, sales_executive, accounts_executive} only, (b) flipped six guards in /app/backend/routers/sales_router.py from require_permission('quotations','read') to require_permission('sales_reports','read'), (c) updated Layout.jsx nav-sales-reports perm prop accordingly, (d) added GET /api/admin/role-preview/{role} for live workspace preview in Add User. All 25 backend tests + dialog UI tests pass.
